data.content:
Implementation review — APPROVED.

Artifact reviewed: main branch of both repos at commits
- knots: fbfe76f, b8b9759, d5ecb30
- foolery: c2756326, d23064d2, 68a3a881

Verified against AC1–AC8:
- AC1 docs reframed (docs/leases.md + README.md in knots; docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md + adr-knots-compatibility.md in foolery). "fallback" absent in the identity context across all four.
- AC2 TAXONOMY.md clean; no "or_else" or "fallback" describing identity derivation in code comments.
- AC3 deprecation warning helper (helpers.rs:32) called from every non-lease-create sink. 9 tests in tests_lease_deprecation.rs assert warnings + ignore semantics. Grammar handles singular and plural (d5ecb30).
- AC4 lease-sourced identity verified at every sink: execute_next, execute_rollback, execute_claim, execute_poll_claim, execute_gate_evaluate, execute_step_annotate, execute_state, build_note_input, build_handoff_input. No-lease case leaves fields unset.
- AC5 [DEPRECATED — IGNORED] prefix on every deprecated flag's help string.
- AC6 poll_claim.rs completion_command uses AGENT_COMPLETION_METADATA_FLAGS = "--actor-kind agent" only; poll_claim.rs:448 asserts the prompt excludes --agent-name. Verified live against fresh binary.
- AC7 Foolery client: createLease is the only place --agent-name/--model/--model-version flow to kno. claimKnot, pollKnot, appendNoteArgs, appendHandoffArgs send none; "foolery" default removed.
- AC8 quality gates green on both repos.

Invariants: no changes to lease lifecycle, SQLite concurrency rules, or workflow state machine. Deprecation warnings emit to stderr and do not alter return codes.

Tests cover required behavior: each sink has at least one positive test (lease identity stamped) and at least one negative test (bogus flag ignored, warning emitted). claim_with_external_lease covers the lease-at-claim-time path; next_without_lease covers the no-lease warning branch.

Sanity gates: cargo fmt clean, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean, cargo test 1111+ tests passing, coverage 95.14%.

No security, performance, or maintainability regressions.

Implementation may advance to shipment.
